blocks.statement: I can compute power as work-per-time, apply P = F·v for instantaneous power, and convert between Watts and horsepower.

blocks.whatWeKnow: Asteroid impact delivers HUGE energy in MICROSECONDS — that's the catastrophic POWER.
blocks.connection: It's not just the energy. The same energy delivered slowly (a billion years) vs. fast (a millisecond) means lava flow vs. extinction event.

blocks.markdown:
## How fast can you spend energy?

Work tells you HOW MUCH energy moves. Power tells you HOW FAST:

**P = W / t** — units: Watts (1 W = 1 J/s)

When a force pushes something moving at speed v, there's an instantaneous form:

**P = F · v** (from P = W/t = F·d/t = F·v)

**Horsepower:** 1 hp ≈ 746 W. A 100-hp engine ≈ 75,000 W. Multiply hp by 746 to get Watts; divide Watts by 746 to get hp.

Same work, different power: climbing the stairs in 5 s or in 50 s moves the SAME energy — the 5-s climb just needs 10× the power.

terms.definition: The rate of energy transfer. P = W / t. Units: Watts (1 W = 1 J/s). A 700-N person climbing a 4-m staircase in 5 s: P = (700·4)/5 = 560 W.
terms.cognate: Sp. potencia · Pt. potência · HC pisans

terms.definition: An instantaneous form of power: force times the speed of the point of application, derived from P = W/t = F·d/t = F·v. A car engine at 75 kW propelling the car at 27 m/s: F = P/v = 75,000/27 ≈ 2,780 N. Use P = F·v when you know force and speed simultaneously; use P = W/t when you know work over time.
terms.cognate: Sp. potencia instantánea · Pt. potência instantânea

blocks.title: Power is NOT energy
blocks.markdown: Power is the RATE of energy transfer. Big power = fast transfer — not necessarily a lot of energy. Two devices can do the same work; the more powerful one just does it faster.

blocks.prompt: CLIMBING STAIRS. A 700-N student climbs a 4-m staircase in 5 s. What's their power output?
blocks.answer: 560 W — about 5.6 light-bulbs of power, briefly.

blocks.prompt: CAR ENGINE POWER. A car engine outputs 100 hp (about 75,000 W) while driving at 27 m/s (≈ 60 mph). What's the driving force?
blocks.answer: ≈ 2,780 N of driving force — balanced by drag + rolling resistance at steady speed.

blocks.title: Asteroid impact as a power event
blocks.markdown: Asteroid impact: ~10¹⁷ J in ~0.1 s. Power ≈ 10¹⁸ W. The Sun outputs ~4×10²⁶ W. For a tenth of a second, an asteroid impact is a millionth-of-the-Sun bright at the impact site.

blocks.prompt: LIFT POWER. A 2-kg ball is lifted 1.5 m in 0.3 s. What power does the lifter generate?
blocks.givenHint: m = 2 kg · h = 1.5 m · t = 0.3 s. Weight first: F = mg.

blocks.prompt: Check your lift-power answer, then complete the frame.
blocks.frame: Power is the rate of ___ transfer. Two devices can do the same work, but the one with more power does it ___.

blocks.title: Read & practice
blocks.markdown: Open the reader and read **9.2** and **9.8** (power and the watt; machines, levers, and pulleys). Answer the questions beside the reading as you go.
